Description: Dense inner-city zone of progressive graduates, civil society workers and political activists. Typical of Islington, Hackney, Stokes Croft Bristol. OAC 2021 maps these as Multicultural & Educated Urbanites (Wyszomierski et al. 2023). High graduate density, above-average B1/A2, low manual and deprivation.

BTR and serviced apartment zone near tech/finance districts. EHS private renters: 18% higher, 26% lower managerial NS-SEC. OAC Multicultural Educated Urbanites: high info/finance industry workers. C2 and C1 dominant, A1 moderate. E2 from gig/freelance satellite economy.

Transitional terrace zone with rising C2/B1 inflow displacing D1/F1. OAC 2021 notes sharpening of urban cores as Multicultural Educated Urbanites (Wyszomierski 2023). EHS owner-occupier NS-SEC: 25% higher + 33% lower managerial anchors C2/B1 for incoming layer. F1 residual from incumbent population.

OAC 2011 Hard-Pressed Living: skilled trade, admin, caring/leisure above average. EHS owner-occupier: C2 skilled manual prominent in northern working-class suburbs. ASG C2 nationally 21.3% — elevated to ~28% in this cluster. C1 from NHS/council public sector.

Inner-city zone of artists, musicians and freelance creatives in converted industrial stock. Closest OAC match: Multicultural Educated Urbanites with E2 spike. ASG places these as C1/C2 but income is precarious. Private rent dominant per EHS 2021-22.

Recent migrant and low-wage arrivals zone. OAC 2021 Low-Skilled Migrant and Student Communities: polar distribution between very low education and some graduate migrants. EHS private rent: 15% semi-routine + 11% routine anchors E1/F1 dominance. Overcrowding elevated per IMD housing dimension.

Post-war flatted social estate. EHS 2021-22 directly anchors: social renters 30% semi-routine, 23% routine. ACORN urban adversity 49% of social renters. IMD 2025: North East/West Midlands 54%+ deprived in 1+ dimensions. F1 set at 63%; D1+E1 residual from working tenants.

Prestige residential zone near Westminster, financial districts. ASG AB peaks in London at 28.4% nationally; elite IC zones exceed this. A1 set at 35% anchored by ASG Grade A (top of AB). EHS owner-occupied: 25% higher + 33% lower managerial gives A1+B1+C2 dominant.

Dense inner-city zone of progressive graduates, civil society workers and political activists. Typical of Islington, Hackney, Stokes Croft Bristol. OAC 2021 maps these as Multicultural & Educated Urbanites (Wyszomierski et al. 2023). High graduate density, above-average B1/A2, low manual and deprivation.

OAC 2021 Suburbanites supergroup: higher qualifications, semi/detached owner-occupied, info/finance/public admin sectors. EHS owner-occupier: 33% lower managerial NS-SEC anchors C1 at 34%. ASG C1 nationally 32.8% — this cluster is C1-dominant per pen portrait.

Young working private renters. EHS private renters: C1 26% lower managerial dominant but 15% semi-routine + 11% routine = D1/E1 significant. Financially stretched ACORN category. C1/E1 co-dominant; moderate F1 from precarious fringe.

Outer creative/freelance zone. E2 elevated from creative economy spillover. Wyszomierski 2023 notes BTR and creative fringe expansion in outer zones. C2 from established professional settlers. F1 moderate from mixed-income private rent.

Peripheral post-war social estate. EHS 2021-22 social renters: 30% semi-routine + 23% routine. ACORN urban adversity 49% of social renters. IMD 2025 employment deprivation concentrated in North East/West Midlands. F1 set at 59%; D1+E1 residual from working tenants.

OAC 2021 Retired Professionals in rural context — Wyszomierski 2023: formerly Rural Residents now reclassified as Retired Professionals. Detached, outright owned, 2 cars. A1/B1/C2 reflect former professional occupations of retirees. Service workers support local economy. F1 from pension-poor rural elderly.

OAC Peri-Urbanites / Suburbanites in rural fringe context per Wyszomierski 2023. EHS first-time buyer: C1/C2 dominant. Car-dependent per OAC rural pen portrait (2+ cars). Dual income young families. F1 suppressed by mortgage filter.

OAC Rural & Outlying Areas with agriculture/land management employment spike. D1 from estate workers; A1 from landowner/estate director; E1 from self-employed rural trades. Consistent with Rural Outlying pen portrait characteristics.

OAC Rural & Outlying Areas pen portrait: higher proportion in agriculture, detached properties, owner-occupied, 2+ cars. EHS rural: D1 in skilled trade/agriculture dominant. E1 from farm self-employment (NS-SEC L8-L9). F1 suppressed by owner-occupation but rural hidden poverty present per IMD.

OAC Hard-Pressed Communities in rural context (Welsh valleys, Durham pit villages). IMD 2025: employment deprivation domain highest in post-industrial areas. EHS social renters: 53% semi-routine/routine. F1 elevated per IMD employment + health deprivation dimensions.

OAC 2021 Retired Professionals — key 2021 new supergroup per Wyszomierski 2023: prevalent in former Suburbanite and Rural Resident neighbourhoods. High asset ownership. A1/B1/C2 reflect former occupations. Service workers (C1/D1/E1) support retirees. F1 from pension-poor minority.

University-adjacent suburb. OAC Suburbanites with B1 spike from academic employment. Wyszomierski 2023: educational/occupational outcomes foreground in these zones. A2 elevated from civic/political activism. ASG AB above national average.

OAC Suburbanites core pen portrait: higher qualifications, owner-occupied, info/finance/public admin employment. EHS owner-occupier: 25% higher + 33% lower managerial = A1+B1+C2 dominant. ASG AB exceeds national 23.3% in professional suburban belts. F1 near-zero.

New-build suburban estate. OAC Suburbanites younger profile per Wyszomierski 2023 Young Family subgroup. EHS first-time buyer NS-SEC: C1/C2 dominant. Dual income household. Mortgage-anchored owner-occupation suppresses F1.

Suburban trade and self-employment zone. NS-SEC L8-L9 (E1) and L10-L11 (D1) elevated. EHS: C2 owner-occupied prominent outside London. Consistent with Hard-Pressed Living pen portrait characteristics of skilled trade employment.

Tech campus and startup zone. OAC 2021 Multicultural Educated Urbanites with information industry employment spike. ASG AB elevated: London 28.4% nationally but tech hubs exceed this. B1+A1+C2 dominant. E2 from freelance tech/creative economy. Low D1/F1.

New towns: OAC Suburbanites and Peri-Urbanites mix. EHS: public sector employment prominent (C1 = nurses, teachers, council). D1 from manufacturing/trade original base. F1 at national average consistent with planned mixed-income intent.

OAC Peri-Urbanites / Suburbanites in market town context. EHS owner-occupier NS-SEC: 25% higher + 33% lower managerial. IMD: market towns typically IMD quintile 3-4. ASG AB above national average. A1/B1/C2 dominant from local professional base.

OAC 2021 Retired Professionals supergroup in coastal context — Wyszomierski 2023 identifies this as key new 2021 category in coastal/outlying areas. IDAOPI elevated. Working-age service economy (C1/D1/E1) supports retirees. F1 from pension poverty.

Largest rural settlement. OAC Rural & Outlying Areas with service function. Broad NS-SEC mix per EHS rural data. D1 from agriculture/trade; C1 from public services; E1 from self-employed rural traders; moderate F1 from rural hidden poverty (IMD 2025 rural deprivation domain).

OAC Hard-Pressed Communities in coastal context. IMD 2025: coastal towns among most deprived LSOAs. EHS social renters: 53% semi-routine/routine. ACORN urban adversity. Seasonal E1 from tourism/hospitality. F1 at 52% consistent with IMD bottom quintile concentration.

Zone surrounding major university campus. OAC 2021 'Low-Skilled Migrant and Student Communities' captures the polar distribution of educational outcomes. EHS 2021-22 confirms private rent dominant. B2 peaks at 45%; B1 elevated from academic staff; F1 from low-income non-student residents.

Zone surrounding major university campus. OAC 2021 'Low-Skilled Migrant and Student Communities' captures the polar distribution of educational outcomes. EHS 2021-22 confirms private rent dominant. B2 peaks at 45%; B1 elevated from academic staff; F1 from low-income non-student residents.
